clusters.title: WHO director arrives in Canary Islands to oversee hantavirus cruise evacuation
clusters.neutral_headline: Worldwide race to trace passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship
bullet_summary: There are nine confirmed or suspected cases connected to the ship, including three deaths, health officials said
The European Union is sending two more planes for the remaining European citizens, and the US and UK have also confirmed planes and contingency plans for non-EU citizens
A total of 8 cases, including 3 deaths, have been reported as of Friday
Six of these cases cases are confirmed as Andes virus and four patients are currently hospitalised

items.title: WHO director arrives in Canary Islands to oversee hantavirus cruise evacuation
items.standfirst: More than 100 people from a cruise ship dealing with an outbreak of the rare and deadly hantavirus are set to be disembarked.
items.content: More than 100 people from a cruise ship dealing with an outbreak of the rare and deadly hantavirus are set to be disembarked.

items.title: Britons on virus-hit cruise ship to isolate at hospital after being flown to UK
items.standfirst: The MV Hondius is expected to dock in the Canary Islands this weekend.
items.content: The MV Hondius is expected to dock in the Canary Islands this weekend.

items.title: European nations dispatch planes to evacuate citizens from hantavirus-hit cruise ship
items.standfirst: Germany, France, Belgium, Ireland, and the Netherlands will send planes to evacuate their citizens aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship ​hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak, Spain's interior minister said on Saturday, adding that transport will also be arranged by the European Union, the US and the UK for the remaining passengers.
items.content: Germany, France, Belgium, Ireland, and the Netherlands will send planes to evacuate their citizens aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship ​hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak, Spain's interior minister said on Saturday, adding that transport will also be arranged by the European Union, the US and the UK for the remaining passengers.

items.title: What people need to know about hantavirus
items.standfirst: Dr. Céline Gounder joins "CBS Saturday Morning" to explain the real risks and realities of hantavirus amid an outbreak on a cruise ship.
items.content: Dr. Céline Gounder joins "CBS Saturday Morning" to explain the real risks and realities of hantavirus amid an outbreak on a cruise ship.

items.title: Tenerife prepares to evacuate hantavirus cruise ship
items.standfirst: Passengers with symptoms could be sent to isolation on the island, health officials warn
items.content: Passengers with symptoms could be sent to isolation on the island, health officials warn

items.title: Spain readies for hantavirus cruise ship to dock, evacuate passengers on Canary Islands
items.standfirst: The cruise ship dealing with a deadly outbreak of hantavirus is set to dock at Spain's Canary Islands and evacuate passengers and crew. There are nine confirmed or suspected cases connected to the ship, including three deaths, health officials said.
items.content: The cruise ship dealing with a deadly outbreak of hantavirus is set to dock at Spain's Canary Islands and evacuate passengers and crew. There are nine confirmed or suspected cases connected to the ship, including three deaths, health officials said.

items.title: Spanish island braces for hantavirus cruise ship as WHO urges calm amid protests
items.standfirst: TENERIFE, Spain — The head of the World Health Organization on Saturday made an appeal to locals in Tenerife to remain calm as the Spanish island prepared to receive passengers from a hantavirus-hit cruise ship that has left three people dead and sparked growing local outrage
items.content: TENERIFE, Spain — The head of the World Health Organization on Saturday made an appeal to locals in Tenerife to remain calm as the Spanish island prepared to receive passengers from a hantavirus-hit cruise ship that has left three people dead and sparked growing local outrage

items.title: Contact tracing begins after Hantavirus-hit cruise passengers disembarks in St. Helena
items.standfirst: Health authorities have launched international contact tracing efforts following three deaths aboard a cruise ship linked to suspected hantavirus exposure. Officials are monitoring passengers who disembarked in St. Helena, while suspected cases in Alicante and Barcelona remain under observation pending test results. FRANCE 24's Sarah Morris reports from Madrid.
items.content: Health authorities have launched international contact tracing efforts following three deaths aboard a cruise ship linked to suspected hantavirus exposure. Officials are monitoring passengers who disembarked in St. Helena, while suspected cases in Alicante and Barcelona remain under observation pending test results. FRANCE 24's Sarah Morris reports from Madrid.

items.title: ‘This is not another Covid,’ WHO chief tells Tenerife as hantavirus cruise ship heads to island – as it happened
items.standfirst: This live blog is now closedThe interior minister of Spain told Reuters on Saturday that Germany, France, Belgium, Ireland and the Netherlands have confirmed they will send planes to repatriate nationals from their respective countries aboard the cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak.The European Union is sending two more planes for the remaining European citizens, and the US and UK have also confirmed planes and contingency plans for non-EU citizens.A total of 8 cases, including 3 deaths, have been reported as of Friday. Six of these cases cases are confirmed as Andes virus and four patients are currently hospitalised.One case previously reported as suspected hantavirus has now been reclassified as a non-case after testing negative for Andes (ANDV) virus.A man who disembarked in Tristan da Cunha on 14 April is currently stable and in isolation. He is currently a probable case until laboratory confirmation.Passengers who travelled on the same flight from St Helena to South Africa along with one of the confirmed cases have been contacted – 75 of those contacts have been identified in South Africa, of whom 42 have been traced by national authorities and are currently under monitoring. Continue reading...
items.content: This live blog is now closedThe interior minister of Spain told Reuters on Saturday that Germany, France, Belgium, Ireland and the Netherlands have confirmed they will send planes to repatriate nationals from their respective countries aboard the cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak.The European Union is sending two more planes for the remaining European citizens, and the US and UK have also confirmed planes and contingency plans for non-EU citizens.A total of 8 cases, including 3 deaths, have been reported as of Friday. Six of these cases cases are confirmed as Andes virus and four patients are currently hospitalised.One case previously reported as suspected hantavirus has now been reclassified as a non-case after testing negative for Andes (ANDV) virus.A man who disembarked in Tristan da Cunha on 14 April is currently stable and in isolation. He is currently a probable case until laboratory confirmation.Passengers who travelled on the same flight from St Helena to South Africa along with one of the confirmed cases have been contacted – 75 of those contacts have been identified in South Africa, of whom 42 have been traced by national authorities and are currently under monitoring. Continue reading...

items.title: 5/9: Saturday Morning
items.standfirst: A Frontier plane struck a pedestrian on the runway in Denver before takeoff. Meanwhile, the cruise ship with a hantavirus outbreak is set to evacuate passengers on Spain's Canary Islands.
items.content: A Frontier plane struck a pedestrian on the runway in Denver before takeoff. Meanwhile, the cruise ship with a hantavirus outbreak is set to evacuate passengers on Spain's Canary Islands.

items.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship heads for Canary Islands to evacuate passengers
items.standfirst: Spanish authorities on Friday were preparing to receive more than 140 passengers and crew members on board a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship headed for the Canary Islands, where health officials have said they will perform careful evacuations. The vessel is expected to arrive Sunday at the Spanish island of Tenerife, the passengers will then be transported to the airport and flown back to their respective countries.
items.content: Spanish authorities on Friday were preparing to receive more than 140 passengers and crew members on board a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship headed for the Canary Islands, where health officials have said they will perform careful evacuations. The vessel is expected to arrive Sunday at the Spanish island of Tenerife, the passengers will then be transported to the airport and flown back to their respective countries.

items.title: U.S. plans evacuation for Americans on cruise ship in hantavirus outbreak
items.standfirst: The MV Hondius is currently traveling to the Canary Islands, where the 147 people on board will be methodically off-boarded and flown home.
items.content: The MV Hondius is currently traveling to the Canary Islands, where the 147 people on board will be methodically off-boarded and flown home.

items.title: 7 states prepare to receive Americans possibly exposed to hantavirus
items.standfirst: The U.S. has entered emergency response mode as a cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak sails toward one of Spain’s Canary Islands.
items.content: The U.S. has entered emergency response mode as a cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak sails toward one of Spain’s Canary Islands.

items.title: New plan to evacuate Americans from Hantavirus cruise
items.standfirst: The State Department is arranging a flight from the Canary Islands back to the U.S., where the 17 Americans on board the cruise ship will isolate at the national quarantine unit at Nebraska Medicine in Omaha. NBC News’ Daniele Hamamdjian reports.
items.content: The State Department is arranging a flight from the Canary Islands back to the U.S., where the 17 Americans on board the cruise ship will isolate at the national quarantine unit at Nebraska Medicine in Omaha. NBC News’ Daniele Hamamdjian reports.

items.title: Worldwide race to trace passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship
items.standfirst: About 12 countries have so far been linked to the hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius, as the UN health agency confirms at least five cases.
items.content: About 12 countries have so far been linked to the hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius, as the UN health agency confirms at least five cases.

items.title: What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak
items.standfirst: Health officials have identified at least nine confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.
items.content: Health officials have identified at least nine confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.

items.title: 17 American passengers aboard hantavirus-hit cruise ship will quarantine in Nebraska
items.standfirst: Seventeen American passengers aboard the cruise ship at the center of a hantavirus outbreak will quarantine at a Nebraska facility that specializes in handling patients with highly communicable diseases, health officials said
items.content: Seventeen American passengers aboard the cruise ship at the center of a hantavirus outbreak will quarantine at a Nebraska facility that specializes in handling patients with highly communicable diseases, health officials said

clusters.title: Putin says Ukrainian conflict nearing end amid Victory Day celebrations
clusters.neutral_headline: Steve Rosenberg: This year's Victory Day parade in Moscow felt very different
bullet_summary: President Vladimir Putin has said he believes the Ukraine war is coming to an end
Reported by CBS News, Sky News World, France24 and others

items.title: Putin says Ukrainian conflict nearing end amid Victory Day celebrations
items.standfirst: The Victory Day celebrations didn't include a show of heavy weapons for the first time in nearly two decades.
items.content: The Victory Day celebrations didn't include a show of heavy weapons for the first time in nearly two decades.

items.title: Putin says Ukraine war likely to end soon - despite Ukrainian battlefield gains
items.standfirst: President Vladimir Putin has said he believes the Ukraine war is coming to an end.
items.content: President Vladimir Putin has said he believes the Ukraine war is coming to an end.

items.title: Putin says Ukraine war 'heading to an end' despite ceasefire violations
items.standfirst: Russia and Ukraine on Saturday accused the other of violating a a three-day US-brokered ceasefire, which coincides with Russia's World War II victory celebrations. Russian President Vladimir Putin, however, asserted at a press conference later that evening that the war was "heading to an end."
items.content: Russia and Ukraine on Saturday accused the other of violating a a three-day US-brokered ceasefire, which coincides with Russia's World War II victory celebrations. Russian President Vladimir Putin, however, asserted at a press conference later that evening that the war was "heading to an end."

items.title: 'Russia's scaled-down Victory Day parade felt more like a display of weakness for Vladimir Putin'
Steve Rosenberg: This year's Victory Day parade in Moscow felt very different
items.standfirst: The BBC's Russia editor reports from a Red Square with none of the military hardware which the Kremlin normally showcases to project power internationally.
items.content: The BBC's Russia editor reports from a Red Square with none of the military hardware which the Kremlin normally showcases to project power internationally.

items.title: Moscow marks Victory Day amid tight security
items.standfirst: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday voiced confidence of victory in Ukraine as he oversaw a military parade on Red Square commemorating the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II – a show that didn't include heavy weapons for the first time in nearly two decades. Security was tight in Moscow as Putin and several foreign leaders attended the parade, which was scaled down even as a US-brokered three-day ceasefire eased concerns about possible Ukrainian attempts to disrupt the festivities.
items.content: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday voiced confidence of victory in Ukraine as he oversaw a military parade on Red Square commemorating the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II – a show that didn't include heavy weapons for the first time in nearly two decades. Security was tight in Moscow as Putin and several foreign leaders attended the parade, which was scaled down even as a US-brokered three-day ceasefire eased concerns about possible Ukrainian attempts to disrupt the festivities.

items.title: Russia will always be victorious, says Putin at scaled-back Victory Day parade
items.standfirst: Moscow blanketed in heavy security despite last-minute announcement of three-day ceasefire with UkraineVladimir Putin has declared Russia will always be victorious as he oversaw a scaled-back Victory Day parade on Red Square held under heavy security amid mounting fears of Ukrainian attacks and growing public fatigue with the war.Speaking to the crowd, the Russian leader invoked the sacrifices of the second world war to rally support for his soldiers fighting in the war in Ukraine. “The great feat of the generation of victors inspires the warriors carrying out the tasks of the special military operation today,” he said, using the Kremlin’s preferred euphemism for his invasion of Ukraine. Continue reading...
items.content: Moscow blanketed in heavy security despite last-minute announcement of three-day ceasefire with UkraineVladimir Putin has declared Russia will always be victorious as he oversaw a scaled-back Victory Day parade on Red Square held under heavy security amid mounting fears of Ukrainian attacks and growing public fatigue with the war.Speaking to the crowd, the Russian leader invoked the sacrifices of the second world war to rally support for his soldiers fighting in the war in Ukraine. “The great feat of the generation of victors inspires the warriors carrying out the tasks of the special military operation today,” he said, using the Kremlin’s preferred euphemism for his invasion of Ukraine. Continue reading...

items.title: Putin denounces Nato at scaled-back Victory Day parade
items.standfirst: The Russian leader used his annual speech to justify his so-called special military operation in Ukraine.
items.content: The Russian leader used his annual speech to justify his so-called special military operation in Ukraine.

items.title: Rosenberg: Scaled-back Victory Parade in Moscow's Red Square
items.standfirst: The BBC's Russia editor reports from Moscow, where the annual Victory Day display was quieter than usual.
items.content: The BBC's Russia editor reports from Moscow, where the annual Victory Day display was quieter than usual.

items.title: Moscow marks Victory Day with a Red Square parade under tight security
items.standfirst: Security was tight in Moscow as Putin and several foreign leaders attended the parade, even as a U.S.-brokered three-day ceasefire eased concerns about possible Ukrainian attempts to disrupt the festivities.
items.content: Security was tight in Moscow as Putin and several foreign leaders attended the parade, even as a U.S.-brokered three-day ceasefire eased concerns about possible Ukrainian attempts to disrupt the festivities.(Image credit: Pavel Bednyakov)

items.title: Russia marks Victory Day with scaled-back parade
items.standfirst: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday voiced confidence of victory in Ukraine as he oversaw a military parade on Red Square commemorating the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II. But this year, for the first time in nearly two decades, the parade took place without tanks, missiles and other heavy weapons, aside from a traditional flyover of combat jets over fears of an Ukrainian attack.
items.content: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday voiced confidence of victory in Ukraine as he oversaw a military parade on Red Square commemorating the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II. But this year, for the first time in nearly two decades, the parade took place without tanks, missiles and other heavy weapons, aside from a traditional flyover of combat jets over fears of an Ukrainian attack.

items.title: Russia is fighting an 'aggressive' NATO-backed force, Putin says in Victory Day speech
items.standfirst: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Saturday that Russian troops in Ukraine were fighting an "aggressive force" backed by all of NATO and described his war goals as "just" in a combative speech during the annual Victory Day parade on Red Square.
items.content: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Saturday that Russian troops in Ukraine were fighting an "aggressive force" backed by all of NATO and described his war goals as "just" in a combative speech during the annual Victory Day parade on Red Square.

items.title: North Korean troops join Putin’s scaled-back parade as Ukraine agrees temporary ceasefire
items.standfirst: There were no neat rows of tanks or ballistic missile carriers rolling down the smooth cobbles of Moscow’s Red Square on Saturday, as Russia scaled back what is usually a grand militaristic Victory Day parade celebrating the Soviet Union’s role in defeating Nazi Germany
items.content: There were no neat rows of tanks or ballistic missile carriers rolling down the smooth cobbles of Moscow’s Red Square on Saturday, as Russia scaled back what is usually a grand militaristic Victory Day parade celebrating the Soviet Union’s role in defeating Nazi Germany

clusters.title: Péter Magyar sworn in as Hungary’s prime minister ending 16-year Orbán era
clusters.neutral_headline: Péter Magyar sworn in as Hungary’s prime minister ending 16-year Orbán era
bullet_summary: "It's officially a new era", said Zsolt Kerner, senior reporter at Hungarian online media 24
Reported by Guardian World, Al Jazeera, BBC World and others

items.title: Péter Magyar sworn in as Hungary’s prime minister ending 16-year Orbán era
items.standfirst: Jubilation in Budapest as new leader invites people to ‘step through gate of regime change’The pro-European centre-right leader Péter Magyar has been sworn in as prime minister of Hungary, marking the official end to Viktor Orbán’s 16 years in power.Saturday’s ceremony – during which Magyar had invited people to join him to “write Hungarian history” together and “step through the gate of regime change” – comes a month after his opposition Tisza party won a landslide victory in parliamentary elections. Continue reading...
items.content: Jubilation in Budapest as new leader invites people to ‘step through gate of regime change’The pro-European centre-right leader Péter Magyar has been sworn in as prime minister of Hungary, marking the official end to Viktor Orbán’s 16 years in power.Saturday’s ceremony – during which Magyar had invited people to join him to “write Hungarian history” together and “step through the gate of regime change” – comes a month after his opposition Tisza party won a landslide victory in parliamentary elections. Continue reading...

items.title: Péter Magyar Takes Office, Marking the Official End of the Orbán Era
items.standfirst: Peter Magyar has officially been sworn in as Hungary’s new prime minister, almost a month after defeating Viktor Orban.
items.content: Peter Magyar has officially been sworn in as Hungary’s new prime minister, almost a month after defeating Viktor Orban.

items.title: I will serve - not rule over Hungary, says new PM
items.standfirst: Nearly a month ago Péter Magyar steered his Tisza party to a landslide victory, sweeping away 16 years of rule by Viktor Orbán.
items.content: Nearly a month ago Péter Magyar steered his Tisza party to a landslide victory, sweeping away 16 years of rule by Viktor Orbán.

items.title: Péter Magyar sworn in as Hungary's prime minister, ending Viktor Orbán's rule
items.standfirst: Péter Magyar's center-right Tisza party defeated Orbán's nationalist-populist Fidesz in a stunning blow last month.
items.content: Péter Magyar's center-right Tisza party defeated Orbán's nationalist-populist Fidesz in a stunning blow last month.

items.title: Hungary’s new prime minister sworn in after landslide defeat of Viktor Orban
items.standfirst: Centre-right leader Peter Magyar has been sworn in as Hungary’s new prime minister.
items.content: Centre-right leader Peter Magyar has been sworn in as Hungary’s new prime minister.

items.title: Peter Magyar sworn in as Hungary’s PM, ending Orban’s 16 years in power
items.standfirst: Magyar's Tisza party secured a huge parliamentary majority in last month's election.
items.content: Magyar's Tisza party secured a huge parliamentary majority in last month's election.

items.title: 'Clean break' with Orban: Hungary's Magyar to usher in 'new era'
items.standfirst: Hungary's incoming prime minister Pater Magyar is marking a "clean break" with his predecessor Viktor Orban as he is set to be sworn into office on Saturday. "It's officially a new era", said Zsolt Kerner, senior reporter at Hungarian online media 24.hu. Magyar was previously a member of Orban's Fidesz party.
items.content: Hungary's incoming prime minister Pater Magyar is marking a "clean break" with his predecessor Viktor Orban as he is set to be sworn into office on Saturday. "It's officially a new era", said Zsolt Kerner, senior reporter at Hungarian online media 24.hu. Magyar was previously a member of Orban's Fidesz party.

items.title: Magyar to succeed Orban as Hungary's new prime minister
items.standfirst: Pro-European conservative Peter Magyar arrived at Hungary's parliament Saturday morning to be sworn in as prime minister, on a promise of "regime change" after nationalist Viktor Orban's 16 years in power. The former government insider-turned-critic scored a landslide victory last month, pledging wide-ranging reforms to fight corruption.
items.content: Pro-European conservative Peter Magyar arrived at Hungary's parliament Saturday morning to be sworn in as prime minister, on a promise of "regime change" after nationalist Viktor Orban's 16 years in power. The former government insider-turned-critic scored a landslide victory last month, pledging wide-ranging reforms to fight corruption.

items.title: Hungary’s Magyar set to be sworn in as new prime minister
items.standfirst: Hungary's incoming prime minister, Peter Magyar, arrived Saturday at the Parliament building to be sworn into office, ending Viktor Orban's autocratic 16-year rule. RFI Correspondent Florence Le Bruyère reports from Budapest.
items.content: Hungary's incoming prime minister, Peter Magyar, arrived Saturday at the Parliament building to be sworn into office, ending Viktor Orban's autocratic 16-year rule. RFI Correspondent Florence Le Bruyère reports from Budapest.

clusters.title: Pedestrian fatally struck by Frontier plane departing Denver for LA
clusters.neutral_headline: Frontier Airlines plane fatally strikes person during takeoff at Denver airport
bullet_summary: A pedestrian was hit by a Frontier airplane departing Denver for LA late Friday night, the airport and airline confirmed
The plane, headed to Los Angeles, “reported striking a pedestrian during takeoff” at about 11
Denver International Airport said the person had jumped a fence and dashed into the aircraft's path minutes before being struck
Pilots were forced to cancel the takeoff to Los Angeles after smoke was reported in the cabin and the pedestrian was killed, the airport also said

items.title: Frontier Airlines plane fatally strikes person during takeoff at Denver airport
items.standfirst: Passengers evacuated safely after person jumped perimeter fence and walked on to runway, airport spokesperson saysA Frontier Airlines plane hit and killed a person on the runway of Denver’s international airport during takeoff, sparking an engine fire and forcing passengers to evacuate, authorities said.The plane, headed to Los Angeles, “reported striking a pedestrian during takeoff” at about 11.19pm on Friday, the Denver airport’s official X account wrote. items.title: Frontier plane fatally hits pedestrian on Denver runway

Pilots were forced to cancel the takeoff to Los Angeles after smoke was reported in the cabin and the pedestrian was killed, the airport also said.

items.title: Stalemate lingers as Iran waits to respond to latest U.S. peace proposal
items.standfirst: No breakthroughs to end the war with Iran have been made as the U.S. awaits Iran’s response to its latest peace proposal. Despite exchanging fire this week, both sides are maintaining that the month-long ceasefire still holds. NBC News’ Julie Tsikrin has the latest.
items.content: No breakthroughs to end the war with Iran have been made as the U.S. awaits Iran’s response to its latest peace proposal. Despite exchanging fire this week, both sides are maintaining that the month-long ceasefire still holds. NBC News’ Julie Tsikrin has the latest.

items.title: US awaits Iran's response to latest proposal amid technical ceasefire

items.title: Man arrested after five people were hit by car in Nottinghamshire town
items.standfirst: One man critically injured following incident in Arnold and suspect is being held on suspicion of attempted murderA man has been arrested on suspicion of attempted murder after five people were struck by a car in a town centre in Nottinghamshire, police have said.One man suffered life-threatening injuries and remains critically ill in hospital while four other men sustained less serious injuries after the incident in Arnold shortly before 1.10am on Saturday. items.content: Greek media say the drone, which reportedly had explosives, could be either Ukrainian or linked to the Russia-Ukraine war.

clusters.title: Parent company of Trump’s Truth Social site reports $400m loss this year
clusters.neutral_headline: Parent company of Trump’s Truth Social site reports $400m loss this year
bullet_summary: Reported by Al Jazeera, Bloomberg Technology

items.title: Parent company of Trump’s Truth Social site reports $400m loss this year
items.standfirst: Losses driven largely by depreciation of cryptocurrency assets as prices of digital currencies tumble.
items.content: Losses driven largely by depreciation of cryptocurrency assets as prices of digital currencies tumble.

items.title: Trump Media Posts $405 Million Loss Driven by Crypto Holdings
items.standfirst: The Trump family media group posted a net drop of $405.9 million in the first quarter, largely driven by unrealized losses in cryptocurrencies held by the company.
items.content: The Trump family media group posted a net drop of $405.9 million in the first quarter, largely driven by unrealized losses in cryptocurrencies held by the company.

clusters.title: Russia and Ukraine begin 3-day ceasefire as tensions ramp up between U.S. and Iran
clusters.neutral_headline: Three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine announced
bullet_summary: President Trump said Friday that fighting would halt between Russia and Ukraine for three days
Reported by CBS News, Sky News World

items.title: Russia and Ukraine begin 3-day ceasefire as tensions ramp up between U.S. and Iran
items.standfirst: President Trump said Friday that fighting would halt between Russia and Ukraine for three days. Meanwhile, the U.S. is awaiting Iran's response to a peace proposal.
items.content: President Trump said Friday that fighting would halt between Russia and Ukraine for three days. Meanwhile, the U.S. is awaiting Iran's response to a peace proposal.

items.title: Three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine announced
items.standfirst: A three-day ceasefire has been agreed by the leaders of Russia and Ukraine, as well as an exchange of prisoners, following a request from Donald Trump.
items.content: A three-day ceasefire has been agreed by the leaders of Russia and Ukraine, as well as an exchange of prisoners, following a request from Donald Trump.
